title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast examines social engineering attacks on AI systems, including prompt injection exploits against Meta/Instagram, self-replicating AI worms from University of Toronto, and widespread security failures in nonhuman identity management. The episode questions whether AI agents are more susceptible to manipulation than humans and debates the significance of these emerging threats.
writeup: This IBM Security Intelligence podcast examines whether AI systems can be social engineered, treating AI agents as targets of manipulation in the way humans are. It covers prompt injection exploits against Meta and Instagram, self-replicating AI worms developed at the University of Toronto, and widespread security failures in nonhuman identity management. The discussion weighs whether AI agents are more susceptible to manipulation than people and debates how significant these emerging threats are.
